title: Imperial-class spaceport
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Otheruses|Imperial}}
{{Youmay|the Imperial-class starport|the [[Standard Class starport|standard-class starport]] or the [[stellar-class starport]]|linebreak=1}}

An '''Imperial-class starport''',<ref name="AE">''[[Alien Encounters]]''</ref><ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|III|42|Prak City}}</ref> or '''Imperial class starport''',<ref name="GG8">''[[Galaxy Guide 8: Scouts]]''</ref> formerly known as a '''Republic class starport''',<ref name="PSG" /> was a type of [[Spaceport/Legends|starport]].<ref name="GG6" />

==Description==
The Imperial-class [[Spaceport/Legends|starport]] was the best category of starport.<ref name="PSmG">''[[Platt's Smugglers Guide]]''</ref> Modern and quite luxurious,<ref name="GG6" /> this type of port was predominantly found on the most advanced and [[Merchant/Legends|commercial]] [[Planet/Legends|worlds]]. It often sprawled for hundreds of [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] and serviced thousands of [[Starship/Legends|vessels]] a [[Standard day/Legends|day]].<ref name="TRPGSERE">''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]''</ref>

The Imperial-class starport had an impressive array of docking facilities, ship storage, and maintenance areas. It also offered the most lavish amenities for ships' crews and passengers. Its [[Shipyard/Legends|maintenance facilities]] were capable of rapid and high-quality repairs and modifications, though the services may be expensive.<ref name="PSG" />

The Imperial-class starport generally was a major trade center.<ref name="GG8" /> It often was connected to other Imperial-class starports by a major [[Hyperspace route/Legends|trade route]].<ref name="RP">{{JournalCite|2|Rebel Privateers!}}</ref> Many merchants usually maintained their offices at the port, enabling starship [[Captain/Legends|captains]] to conduct their business without ever leaving the confines of the starport.<ref name="PSG" />

The customs office for this type of port was often staffed by highly competent [[Customs official/Legends|officers]] equipped with [[Portable scanner/Legends|portable scanners]].<ref name="GG6" /> Minor infractions were dealt with to the full extent of the law to discourage troublemakers.<ref name="PSG" />

During the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]] usually maintained a significant presence at Imperial-class starports. Starport [[Controller/Legends|control]] and the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] conducted thorough ship and captain identification checks to weed out [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] and other unwelcome [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]].<ref name="PSG" />

During the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], the name of this classification had yet to be changed.<ref name="GG8" />

{{Struct-stub}}

==Behind the scenes==
The Imperial-class starport was created by [[Mark Rein-Hagen]] and [[Stewart Wieck]] for ''[[Galaxy Guide 6: Tramp Freighters]]'', a [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] sourcebook published in [[1990]] by [[West End Games]] for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="GG6" />
